entry.title: KFC Tests New Zinger Sandwich
entry.summary:
KFC is testing its new Zinger Sandwich at participating locations throughout California.
The new sandwich is meant to be a spicier, more flavorful version of the Zinger Sandwich launched (and rather quickly discontinued) in 2017. The new Zinger Sandwich features a "spicy marinated chicken breast with that Extra Crispy crunch, shredded lettuce, and creamy Colonel’s Mayo on a toasted brioche bun." Compared to the previous version, KFC has changed the recipe for the chicken breast filet and swapped out the sesame seed bun for the toasted brioche bun. It's interesting to note that, if it makes it out of testing, the Zinger Sandwich would be the only menu item containing lettuce on the KFC menu. Prices vary, but the sandwich costs $6.49 at a local KFC here in Los Angeles, which is the same price as the chain's Classic Chicken Sandwich. Photo via KFC. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Whataburger Welcomes New Whatadeals Value Menu

Whataburger begins the new year with the launch of a new Whatadeal value menu with items priced at $3, $4, and $5. The lineup includes:
- $3 Big Ranch Wrap - A crispy-fried Whatachick’n Strip, lettuce, tomato, Creamy Buttermilk Ranch, and American cheese folded up in a warm tortilla. - $4 Whatachick’n Bites (5-piece) - Small, crispy-fried chicken breast pieces with gravy or other sauce for dipping. - $5 Bacon and Cheese Whataburger Jr. - A beef patty, bacon, American cheese, lettuce, tomato, pickles, diced onions, and mustard on a 4-inch bun. Photo via Whataburger. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Subway Introduces New Protein Pockets
entry.summary:
Subway introduces new Protein Pockets, which are wraps that offer over 20 grams of protein for $3.99 each. The sandwich chain also launches a new six-inch Sub of the Day for $4.99.
Available for a limited time, Protein Pockets come in four varieties: - Baja Chicken - Grilled chicken strips, Monterey cheddar, Baja Chipotle sauce, lettuce, Roma tomatoes, and jalapenos, wrapped in a 9-inch tortilla and toasted. - Peppercorn Ranch Chicken - Grilled chicken strips, Monterey cheddar, Peppercorn Ranch, lettuce, Roma tomatoes, and pickles, wrapped and toasted in a 9-inch tortilla. - Italian Trio - Black Forest ham, pepperoni, Genoa salami, Monterey cheddar, lettuce, Roma tomatoes, and mayo, wrapped and toasted in a 9-inch tortilla. - Turkey & Ham - Oven-roasted turkey, Black Forest ham, Monterey cheddar, lettuce, Roma tomatoes, and mayo, wrapped and toasted in a 9-inch tortilla. The weekly schedule for the new $4.99 six-inch Sub of the Day is as follows: Additionally, you can get a drink, plus chips or cookies for $2 more. - Meatball Monday - Meatball Marinara - Tuna Tuesday - Classic Tuna - Sweet Onion Wednesday - Sweet Onion Chicken Teriyaki - Turkey Thursday - Oven-Roasted Turkey - Forest Ham Friday - Black Forest Ham - BMT Saturday - Italian B.M.T. - Spicy Italian Sunday - Spicy Italian Finally, Subway updated its MVP Rewards loyalty program to the new Sub Club loyalty program in December. The new program is reminiscent of the chain's past Sub Club sticker cards and offers a free Footlong after you buy three Footlong subs. In comparison, the Sub Club of old required eight stickers/stamps (they gave one sticker for buying a six-inch sub and two stickers for buying a Footlong sub) to redeem for a six-inch sub. Photo via Subway. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Korean-Style Menu Returns to Shake Shack with Two New Additions
entry.summary:
Shake Shack's Korean-Style Menu is back for a limited time and includes two new additions: K-Shack Fried Chicken Bites and the K-Shack Spicy Caramel Shake.
The menu is available now when ordering online through the Shake Shack app or website ahead of a nationwide launch on January 9, 2026. K-Shack Fried Chicken Bites features crispy-fried white-meat chicken bites coated in a spicy-sweet sesame gochujang glaze and sprinkled with fresh scallions. They're served with your choice of dipping sauce. The K-Shack Spicy Caramel Shake is a milkshake made with spicy gochujang caramel frozen custard topped with whipped cream and a drizzle of gochujang caramel. Returning Korean-Style Menu items include: - K-Shack Fried Chicken Sandwich - A crispy-fried chicken breast filet glazed in spicy-sweet sesame gochujang and served with fresh scallions and kimchi slaw made with Choi’s Kimchi, on a toasted potato bun. - K-Shack BBQ Burger - A quarter-pound Angus beef patty (or two or three), American cheese, crispy-fried sweet onions, fresh scallions, and Korean BBQ sauce, served on a toasted potato bun. - K-Shack Spicy BBQ Fries - Crinkle-cut fries dusted with kimchi seasoning and served with a side of Korean BBQ sauce. Cheese Sauce can also be added for an additional charge. Photo via Shake Shack. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Review: KFC - Matty's Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl
entry.summary:
KFC's Matty's Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl features Secret Recipe Fries with gravy, topped with five Kentucky Fried Nuggets and five fried white cheddar Cheese Curds.
I paid $5 for the limited-time bowl. The Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl is KFC's and Chef Matty Matheson's take on poutine.
At first glance, I thought they might have forgotten the gravy in my bowl, but a closer examination revealed that the fries had absorbed much of the gravy. While the fries weren't crispy at all, they were thick enough to hold up well against the gravy. Additionally, the savory flavors of the two meshed very well, resulting in a surprising depth.
On the other hand, the Kentucky Fried Nuggets and the Cheese Curds retained a good deal of crispiness. The chicken nuggets were moist and meaty, but were a bit on the firm side. Flavor-wise, they're meant for dipping and don't offer much in the way of seasoning beyond a rather heavy dose of salt. The Cheese Curds were much better, with a similar flavor to mozzarella but with slightly more flavor. They were somewhat melted inside and, as they cooled, delivered a bit of the squeak that cheese curds are known for. Between the two, I preferred eating the nuggets alone, while the Cheese Curds went well with the fries and gravy.
Overall, the KFC Matty's Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl delivered a delightful take on poutine in a country where poutine is a little bit hard to come by (at least here in Los Angeles) and certainly isn't on offer at any national fast-food chain. Nutritional Info - KFC Matty's Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl Calories - 740 Fat - 39g (Saturated Fat - 11g) Sodium - 2470mg Carbs - 63g (Sugar - less than 1g) Protein - 34g Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Popeyes Brings Back Wraps as Part of New $5 Faves Value Menu
entry.summary:
Popeyes starts off the new year by bringing back Wraps as part of the new $5 Faves Menu.
Available now for a limited time through February 15, 2026, at participating locations nationwide, the $5 Faves Menu is a value menu offering several options for $5 each ($6 each here in Los Angeles). They include:
Three pieces of Signature Chicken (breast pieces cost extra) Three Tenders Two regular sides (Homestyle Mac & Cheese costs extra) Two Wraps
Popeyes Wraps feature a chicken tender wrapped in a soft flour tortilla with shredded lettuce, shredded cheese, and pickles. They come in four varieties: Classic (with a fried chicken tender and mayo), Spicy (with a Classic Tender and spicy mayo), Blackened Classic (with a Blackened Tender and mayo), and Blackened Spicy (with a Blackened Tender and spicy mayo). Photo via Popeyes. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.summary:
Baskin-Robbins introduces new Grahammy ice cream as its latest, limited-time flavor and Flavor of the Month for January 2026.
The new variety features graham cracker-flavored ice cream with chocolate-covered honeycomb pieces and honey graham cracker swirls, which amounts to plenty of honey and graham cracker flavors to go around. The name is a play on the Grammy music awards and text that accompanies the new ice cream flavor reads, "Now this is a winner," and "this flavor is truly music to everyone's ears." entry.title: Checkers & Rally's Puts Together New $5 MVP Meal Deal
entry.summary:
Checkers & Rally's launches the new $5 MVP Meal Deal as their latest value meal offering.
Available for a limited time nationwide, the $5 MVP Meal Deal includes a choice of sandwich, value fries, a small soft drink, and a deep-fried Cinnamon Apple Pie. You can also opt for medium or large fries and a drink for an extra charge (the medium and large MVP Meal Deals cost $6.50 and $7, respectively, at a Rally's here in Los Angeles). The sandwich choices for the value meal include: - Mushroom Swissburger - A beef patty, melted Swiss cheese, and savory mushroom sauce on a toasted, bakery-style bun. - Spicy Chicken Sandwich - A crispy-fried chicken patty, iceberg lettuce, and mayo, served on a toasted bakery-style bun. - Cheese Double - Two beef patties, American cheese, ketchup, mustard, pickles, and onions, served on a toasted bakery-style bun. Photo via Checkers & Rally's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: KFC Brings Back $5 Bowls and Debuts New Cheese Curds
entry.summary:
KFC starts 2026 with the return of $5 Bowls for a limited time. It also debuts new Cheese Curds.
This round of $5 Bowls includes the following options: - New Matty's Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl - Created in partnership with Canadian chef/actor Matty Matheson, the Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl takes inspiration from poutine and features Secret Recipe fries topped with chicken nuggets, fried cheese curds, and brown gravy. - Mac & Cheese Bowl - Homestyle mac & cheese topped with chicken nuggets and a three-cheese blend (cheddar, mozzarella, and pepper jack). - Spicy Mac & Cheese Bowl - Homestyle mac & cheese topped with chicken nuggets, a three-cheese blend, and Nashville Hot Sauce. - Mashed Potato Bowl - Mashed potatoes topped with gravy, corn, chicken nuggets, and a three-cheese blend. - Spicy Mashed Potato Bowl - Mashed potatoes topped with gravy, corn, chicken nuggets, a three-cheese blend, and Nashville Hot Sauce. KFC's new Cheese Curds feature battered and fried Wisconsin-style white cheddar cheese curds. A 10-piece order costs $4.19 near me in Los Angeles. Photo via KFC. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Dairy Queen Welcomes New Oreo Snowdrift Blizzard and Mocha Brownie Mudslide Blizzard
entry.summary:
Dairy Queen launches two new Blizzards this month: the Oreo Snowdrift Blizzard and the Mocha Brownie Mudslide Blizzard. They're joined by the returning Choco-Dipped Strawberry Blizzard.
The Oreo Snowdrift Blizzard is a winter-themed treat that features vanilla soft serve mixed with Oreo cookie pieces and a sky-blue cake batter sauce, topped and stirred with a marshmallow sauce. The Mocha Brownie Mudslide Blizzard is a coffee-inspired dessert that features vanilla soft serve blended with brownie pieces, chocolaty chunks, and coffee. The Choco-Dipped Strawberry Blizzard evokes the warmer days of summer and features vanilla soft serve mixed with strawberry topping and chocolaty chunks. All three Blizzards are available now for a limited time at participating locations nationwide. Photo via Dairy Queen. Read more at Brand Eating!

Available starting January 7, 2026:
Sundae Shoppe No Sugar Added Gelato - $3.79 Simply Nature Organic Tart Cherry Juice - $5.69 Fusia Asian Inspirations Mini Wontons - $6.49 Mama Cozzi's Cauliflower Crust Roasted Veg Pizza - $4.99 Specially Selected Scallops in Sauce - $7.99 Simply Nature Organic Gluten Free Chicken Tenders - $8.99 Clancy's Reduced Fat Kettle Chips - $1.99

Available starting January 21, 2026:
Lunch Buddies Crustless Strawberry PB&J Sandwich - $3.69 Fusia Asian Inspirations Crunchy Rice Rolls - $2.99 Appetitos Crab Rangoon or Cream Cheese Wontons - $3.69 Appetitos Mini Tacos - $5.99 Clancy's Buffalo Wing or Parmesan Garlic Kettle Chips - $1.99
Available starting January 28, 2026:
Kirkwood Southern Chicken Variety Pack - $8.99 Appetitos Breaded Cheese Curds - $5.49 Fremont Fish Market Breaded Crunchy Shrimp - $5.99 Appetitos Pretzel Bites - $2.99

entry.title: Crumbl's Weekly Rotating Menu Through January 10, 2026
entry.summary:
Entering the new year, Crumbl has switched up its weekly rotating dessert menu so that there is now a permanent Classics Menu with six flavors and four rotating "specialties" each week.
From January 5 through January 10, 2026, the limited-time desserts at Crumbl include: - New Dubai Biscoff Brownie - A chocolate brownie topped with a kataifi and Biscoff cookie butter filling, a white drop layer, and a drizzle of Biscoff cookie butter. - New Chocolate Vanilla Ice Cream Swirl Cookie - A brown sugar cookie packed with waffle cone bites, topped with a chocolate-vanilla mousse swirl and more waffle cone bites. - New Oatmeal Marshmallow Cookie - A spiced oatmeal cookie topped with marshmallow cream cheese frosting. - Ultimate Peanut Butter Cookie - A warm peanut butter cookie stuffed with peanut butter and topped with a melted peanut butter drizzle. The permanent Classics Menu includes the following cookies: - Semi-sweet Chocolate Chunk Cookie - A warm cookie filled with semi-sweet chocolate chunks and a sprinkle of flaky sea salt. - Pink Sugar Cookie - An almond sugar cookie topped with a pink almond frosting. - Chocolate Crumb Cookie featuring Oreo - A warm chocolate cookie made with Oreo cookie pieces, topped with vanilla cream cheese frosting and more Oreo cookie pieces. - Brownie Batter Cookie - A warm chocolate cookie packed with semi-sweet chocolate chips and topped with brownie batter. - Snickerdoodle Cookie - A warm vanilla sugar cookie rolled in a cinnamon-sugar coating. - Celebration Cake Cookie - A warm cake batter cookie topped with cake batter cream cheese frosting and rainbow sprinkles. Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Will It Creami? Wendy's Chocolate Frosty
entry.summary:
As I mentioned earlier on the BrandEating Patreon, I received a Ninja Swirl Creami ice cream maker to experiment with courtesy of SharkNinja. I'm going to use it to see how and if various fast-food desserts could be made into ice cream or soft serve. I decided to start off with something easy: Turning Wendy's Chocolate Frosty into soft serve. It's almost soft serve already, so I figured, why not? I'm calling the segment, "Will it Creami?" ("Will it Swirl Creami" just doesn't sound right)
The Process I purchased a large Wendy's Frosty for $3.59 and let it melt slightly to reduce its volume. I then poured it into the Creami's supplied pint container and placed it into the freezer for a little over a day. After it was good and frozen, I ran it through the processing section of the Cremi (it took four minutes), which finely shaves and blends the frozen Frosty into a soft serve consistency (it's like the reverse of your typical ice cream maker, where you churn and freeze the ice cream at the same time). Once it was churned, I attached the container to the soft serve dispensing section of the Creami. There, it was forced/pressed the Frosty soft serve through a nozzle to achieve that signature swirled look. If you don't want/need to have that swirled soft serve appearance, you can skip this step and just spoon the soft serve out of the container.
How it turned out The Wendy's Frosty Soft Serve was pretty good. Obviously, it had the same mild chocolate and hint of vanilla flavor of a regular Frosty, but it also had the firmer, smoother, less melty texture of soft serve (my dispensing skills need improvement, however). It was a little too firm, though, and I wonder if that was due to the relatively low fat content of a Frosty (fat/cream makes ice cream softer) or if I should have given the soft serve another blend (there's a "re-spin" function on the machine). Still, the firmness wasn't outside the range of other soft serves that I've eaten. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Jersey Mike's Offers $10.99 Boardwalk Bundle
entry.summary:
Jersey Mike's introduces a limited-time meal deal called the Boardwalk Bundle.
Priced at $10.99, the Boardwalk Bundle includes a regular #2 sub (AKA the Jersey Shore's Favorite), a small bag of chips, and a 22-oz fountain beverage. The #2 sub consists of provolone, ham, and cappacuolo, plus your choice of veggies, condiments, and bread. Prices vary, but it normally costs $9.95 here in Los Angeles (which amounts to a bag of chips and a drink for $1). The Boardwalk Bundle is available now at participating Jersey Mike's locations. Photo via Jersey Mike's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Arby's Introduces New Meat & 3 Box Meal
entry.summary:
Arby's launches a new discounted value meal called the "Meat & 3 Box."
For $7.99, the Meat & 3 Box includes a Roast Beef or Crispy Chicken Sandwich, three sides (two Mozzarella Sticks, a Peach Cobbler Roll, and small fries), and a small fountain drink. There's also a $9.99 option that swaps in a French Dip & Swiss sandwich in place of the Roast Beef or Crispy Chicken Sandwich. Arby's Meat & 3 Box is available now for a limited time. Photo via Arby's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: PB&J Cookies Return to Insomnia Cookies
entry.summary:
Late-night cookie chain Insomnia Cookies welcomes back two limited-time flavors to kick off 2026: the PB&J Filled Classic Cookie made with Jif and the Fruity Cereal 'N Milk Filled Classic Cookie.
The PB&J Filled Cookie is a peanut butter cookie made with Jif peanut butter and filled with grape jelly. The Fruity Cereal 'N Milk Filled Classic Cookie is a warm, fruity cereal cookie filled with cereal-milk buttercream. Prices vary, but they cost $3.69 each here in Los Angeles. Insomnia Cookies is currently offering a limited-time deal where you can get free delivery on orders of $20+ using the code "DREAMON". Photo via Insomnia Cookies. Read more at Brand Eating!
